title: Cradle-Song At Twilight
content:
The child not yet is lulled to rest.
        Too young a nurse, the slender Night
    So laxly holds him to her breast
        That throbs with flight.

    He plays with her and will not sleep.
        For other playfellows she sighs;
    An unmaternal fondness keep
        Her alien eyes.
meaning:
Alice Christiana Thompson Meynell's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Cradle-Song At Twilight"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
